pi_items.source: Task description and FDA/DailyMed databases confirm Metformin HCl as legitimate drug name
FDA.report confirms NDC 0093-7214-01 is a real Teva Pharmaceuticals product
FDA.report (https://fda.report/NDC/0093-7214 and https://fda.report/NDC/0093-1048) definitively shows NDC 0093-7214-01 is 1000mg metformin HCl, not 500mg. Metformin HCl 500mg is NDC 0093-1048-01.
FDA databases confirm all metformin tablets are measured in milligrams (mg)
DailyMed and FDA.report confirm NDC 0093-7214-01 is a film-coated oral tablet
DailyMed and FDA databases confirm route of administration is Oral for all metformin tablets

privileged_info_summary: This task involves adding a real drug product (Metformin Hydrochloride) to a LibreHealth EHR system. Metformin HCl is a genuine antidiabetic medication. However, there is a critical data integrity issue: the task description specifies 'Metformin HCl 500mg' with NDC number 00093-7214-01, but according to FDA databases, NDC 0093-7214-01 is actually a 1000mg tablet strength. The 500mg strength is NDC 0093-1048-01. The drug form (film-coated tablet) and route (oral) are correctly specified and match the FDA product data. The name pattern 'Metformin' is verified as correct. This represents a genuine contradiction between the task's stated drug strength (500mg) and the specified NDC code (which is 1000mg).
